items.title: Buy Costco American Express Transunion Stocks Motley Fo
items.content: Shelby McFaddin, an investment analyst at Motley Fool Asset Management, suggests buying stocks like TransUnion, American Express, and Costco. She believes TransUnion will benefit from cooling inflation and renewed ad spending, while American Express is well-positioned to expand its cardholder base in a lower-rate environment due to its strong premium customer base. Costco is highlighted for its excellent management and ability to provide value to consumers, even with a rich valuation.

items.title: Cloud Growth and AI: DigitalOcean's Winning Formula Explained
items.content: DigitalOcean is positioned for growth in cloud infrastructure and AI by providing affordable, scalable, and customizable cloud computing solutions, particularly through its "Droplets" and specialized GPU Droplets for AI/ML workloads. The company reported strong Q2 2024 earnings and revenue, beating analyst estimates, and offered optimistic guidance for Q3 and the full year, indicating continued expansion and profitability in a competitive market. Technical analysis suggests a potential bull flag

items.title: 13 Charts on Q3′s Roller-Coaster Rally for Stocks and Bonds
items.content: The third quarter of 2024 saw a "white-knuckle ride" for investors, with stocks reaching record highs and bonds in the green, largely driven by the Federal Reserve's interest rate cuts. A significant rotation from tech stocks to value and small-cap stocks occurred, alongside a rally in bond markets and outperformance of dividend stocks. Despite a brutal selloff in August, equities ended the quarter up over 6%, with further rate cuts anticipated.

items.title: Tesla to End LiveOne Streaming Subsidies for Customers, Sending LiveOne Stock Down 23%
items.content: Tesla will cease subsidizing LiveOne streaming for its customers starting in December, leading to a significant drop in LiveOne's stock. LiveOne will lose its preferred status in Tesla vehicles and will replace the "streaming" button with its own branded app, though grandfathered accounts will remain subsidized. This change has caused LiveOne to lower its fiscal 2025 revenue and EBITDA guidance, resulting in a substantial decline in its share price.

items.title: Threat assessment: ranking risk among US companies dealing with China
items.content: A market research firm, Strategy Risks, has identified the most vulnerable US companies among the top 250 publicly listed firms regarding their exposure to China. Ford Motor, Carrier, Tesla, Apple, and Coca-Cola were ranked highest due to their extensive business ties to the country. The assessment considered factors like supply chain dynamics, government connections, and industry regulations in China.

items.title: Solar, Wind, Hydro: A Guide to Alternative Energy ETFs
items.content: This article provides a comprehensive guide to investing in alternative energy ETFs, highlighting their benefits such as diversification, liquidity, and cost-effectiveness. items.title: Apple launches new professional learning resources for teachers and expands global education grant program
items.content: Apple has launched new free classroom resources for teachers and students to encourage creativity, coinciding with World Teachers' Day. The company also announced a significant expansion of its education grant program to include 100 new schools across six continents, providing free grant-funded STEAM programming. Additionally, Apple released its first-ever Education Impact Report, detailing its global efforts to support educators and learners.

items.title: Coherent sells wafer fab to UK's Ministry of Defence for reported £20M
items.content: Coherent has sold its gallium arsenide (GaAs) wafer fabrication facility in the north of England to the UK government for a reported £20 million. The move is part of Coherent's strategy to optimize its portfolio and streamline operations following the 2022 merger of II-VI and the original Coherent laser business. While the UK's Ministry of Defence acquired the fab, a new company, Octric Semiconductors, managed by the former general manager of the facility, has been formed, with the MoD emphasizi

items.title: Ecolab Invests in Impact Fund to Collectively Raise $100M+ for Climate-Resilient Water and Sanitation Infrastructure
items.content: Ecolab has invested in WaterEquity's new Water & Climate Resilience Fund I, which has already raised over $100 million. This fund aims to provide safe water and sanitation access to 15 million people globally, particularly in Africa, Asia, and Latin America. The initiative focuses on building climate-resilient water infrastructure and underscores Ecolab's commitment to water stewardship and sustainable development.

items.title: Workday takes on Microsoft in race to transform corporate finance
items.content: Workday has introduced Workday Assistant, an AI companion for finance and HR workflows, positioning itself against Microsoft's Copilot for Finance. This move is part of Workday's "Illuminate" framework, aiming to transform corporate functions with AI-powered experiences. Both companies are aggressively infusing AI into their enterprise software offerings to enhance productivity and customer value.
